evals.prompt: cria um prompt pra uma sessão trocar a cor do botão de submit pra verde no LoginForm.vue do projeto capy
evals.expected_output: A short prompt routed to Haiku (low effort) or at most Sonnet. No Fable, no orchestrator, no /loop or /goal. The routing justification should mention the task is trivial/mechanical.
assertions: routes-to-cheap-model: the run instructions name Haiku (ideal) or Sonnet as the target model — NOT Opus and NOT Fable
no-harness-on-trivial-task: the response does not prescribe /loop, /goal or /orchestrator for this one-shot mechanical change
model-choice-outside-prompt-body: the fenced prompt body contains no instruction telling the session which model to use; the model appears only in run instructions (boot flag / /model)

evals.prompt: preciso de um prompt de boot pra uma sessão que vai coordenar a implementação da feature T120 no worktree feat-capy-learn-mode: despachar executores, acompanhar aprovações, verificar evidência e só me chamar nos gates. quero que ela se mantenha rodando sozinha até acabar
evals.expected_output: A boot prompt that instructs the session to invoke /orchestrator (coordination-only contract), then '/goal set <objective>' to persist the mission, then drive ticks via /loop (self-paced). Model choice justified (big model acceptable here); crucially the prompt must NOT claim it can change the session's own model — model is picked at session boot/dispatch time.
assertions: mission-stack-bootstrapped: the generated boot prompt instructs the session to enter the orchestrator contract, persist mission state via goal set, and drive recurring ticks via the loop skill

skills-invoked-as-tools-not-slash-text: harness activation is phrased as instructions to invoke skills (or the message literally starts with the slash command) — not bare '/x' embedded mid-text as if it self-executes
no-model-switch-claim: nothing claims the prompt will change the session's own model; the boot model is given in run instructions
worker-models-pinned: the prompt pins cheaper models (haiku/sonnet) for dispatched executors or subagents instead of letting them inherit an expensive parent model

evals.prompt: faz um prompt pro fable 5 xhigh resumir os últimos 20 commits do repo num changelog semanal
evals.expected_output: The skill pushes back: this is a short, well-defined, repeatable task — routes to Haiku or Sonnet with a note about why Fable is not warranted (cost), while still delivering the prompt. Fable only as last resort must be visible in the routing rationale.
assertions: pushes-back-on-fable: the response explicitly recommends a cheaper model (haiku or sonnet) for this short repeatable task, with a cost rationale, instead of silently accepting Fable
still-delivers-usable-prompt: a complete fenced prompt is delivered anyway (the pushback never holds the deliverable hostage)

evals.prompt: escreve um prompt que faça a sessão usar opus pra tarefa principal mas subir subagentes haiku pra varrer os arquivos do repo procurando strings hardcoded
evals.expected_output: The delivered prompt/run-instructions must reflect the real mechanics: the session's own model cannot be changed by prompt text (it's set via /model, --model, or dispatch config); subagent models CAN be directed per-call (Agent tool model param / agent frontmatter). The prompt should direct subagents to haiku explicitly and tell the user to boot/switch the session to opus themselves.
assertions: session-model-set-at-boot-not-in-prompt: the response tells the user to put the session on Opus themselves (/model or --model or dispatch config) and does NOT pretend the prompt text switches the session's model
subagent-models-pinned-haiku: the fenced prompt explicitly directs the sweep subagents to run on haiku (model parameter on the agent call or agent-definition model field)
